inputs.reasoning: Alternation (kInstAlt) with repetition exercises the switch(arg) cases in the Alt handling, testing both arg==0 and arg==1 paths.
Named captures (kInstCapture) with arg==0 path, exercises capture register logic and stack operations during matching.
Large repetitions create many Push operations that exhaust the job stack, forcing GrowStack() to be called and testing njob_ >= maxjob_ condition.
Character ranges and byte matching (kInstByteRange) with optional quantifier tests the if(p < end) branch in byte range matching.
Nested alternations with repetition stress multiple Alt instruction handling and test the default case in switch(arg) for bad arguments.
Multiple sequential repetitions exercise the main loop (while(njob_ > 0)) and CheckAndLoop label execution with ShouldVisit checks.
Multiple captures (kInstCapture with arg==0) test the capture path and Push operations in sequence.
Many optional quantifiers create complex branching that exercises TrySearch loop and opcode switching logic.
POSIX character classes with repetition test byte range matching and character classification in the bitstate engine.
Multiple top-level alternations test the Alt opcode handling with different arg values and exercise the CheckAndLoop path.
